누가복음 13:1.content: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

누가복음 13:2.content: Jesus answered them, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?

누가복음 13:3.content: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

누가복음 13:4.content: Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them; do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

누가복음 13:5.content: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”

누가복음 13:6.content: He spoke this parable. “A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it, and found none.

누가복음 13:7.content: He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down. Why does it waste the soil?’

누가복음 13:8.content: He answered, ‘Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it and fertilize it.

누가복음 13:9.content: If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.’ ”

누가복음 13:10.content: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.

누가복음 13:11.content: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years. She was bent over, and could in no way straighten herself up.

누가복음 13:12.content: When Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”

누가복음 13:13.content: He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight and glorified God.

누가복음 13:14.content: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, “There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!”

누가복음 13:15.content: Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath, and lead him away to water?

누가복음 13:16.content: Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?”

누가복음 13:17.content: As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

누가복음 13:18.content: He said, “What is God’s Kingdom like? To what shall I compare it?

누가복음 13:19.content: It is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and put in his own garden. It grew and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky live in its branches.”

누가복음 13:20.content: Again he said, “To what shall I compare God’s Kingdom?

누가복음 13:21.content: It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour, until it was all leavened.”

누가복음 13:22.content: He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.

누가복음 13:23.content: One said to him, “Lord, are they few who are saved?” He said to them,

누가복음 13:24.content: “Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in and will not be able.

누가복음 13:25.content: When once the master of the house has risen up, and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside and to knock at the door, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’ then he will answer and tell you, ‘I don’t know you or where you come from.’

누가복음 13:26.content: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’

누가복음 13:27.content: He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’

누가복음 13:28.content: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.

누가복음 13:29.content: They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in God’s Kingdom.

누가복음 13:30.content: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”

누가복음 13:31.content: On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, “Get out of here, and go away, for Herod wants to kill you.”

누가복음 13:32.content: He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

누가복음 13:33.content: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can’t be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem.’

누가복음 13:34.content: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!

누가복음 13:35.content: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”
